title: Support Tickets Customer Health Announcements Semantic Contract
description: Coverage-complete semantic contract for the support tickets customer health announcements capability. Semantic information captured as a USF coverage record via the coverage record and equivalence audit; no external source code is imported and the V2 implementation language is an open decision.
aiGuidance: Coverage-complete draft grounded in semantic information (USF coverage record only; no source import; language open). Proof is hermetic/stale lineage, not current readiness. Enrich detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: The support lifecycle creates a tenant support ticket, lists and updates tickets through the support route layer, computes customer health from ticket and usage signals, creates and lists tenant announcements, and exposes health, announcement, and ticket summaries for operators. Support access itself is gated by an adjacent support-approval workflow before a support session is created.

stateModel.description: State includes the support ticket (identifier, subject, body, status, organisation, actor, timestamps), customer-health signals derived from tickets and metered usage, the announcement (identifier, subject, message, creator, created-at), and support-mode approval/session state in the adjacent approval proof. Health is a computed read-model over tickets and usage rather than a stored score.

permissions.description: Support and announcement operations are tenant/operator-scoped through platform support permissions and actor-role metadata, and support-approval access is gated before a support session is created. Tenant context cannot read another tenant's tickets, health, or announcements.

contracts.description: Contracts include support ticket create/list/update operations, a customer-health read operation, tenant announcement create/list operations, and the support-approval request/approve workflow boundary. The approval workflow is the contract boundary that must complete before privileged support access.

validation.description: Validation proves ticket creation returns an identifier, customer health aggregates ticket count and metered usage, announcement creation returns an identifier and listing projects subject/message/creator, and a support session is created only after an approval signal. Inputs are validated at the route/use-case layer before persistence.

errorModel.description: Database or use-case failures surface as route errors, a missing approval remains in a waiting state and does not create support access, and absent ticket/usage rows produce zero-count health signals. The approval gate fails closed so support access is not granted without approval.

auditModel.description: Ticket, announcement, and support-session mutations emit audit metadata through an injected audit port, and support approval records an ordered support-session-created audit lifecycle. Privileged support access is attributable through the approval audit trail.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is live route/use-case availability over relational support tables and metering-derived health signals, with the support-approval proof validating the workflow boundary state rather than assuming support access. Degraded mode disables mutation paths while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by support-ticket and announcement unit tests plus a support-approval runtime proof validating the approval waiting/completed workflow and the support-session audit lifecycle. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No distinct UI capability at the contract level (coverage matrix ui=0); UI is modelled separately and remains a future AI-customised deliverable.
